# terrible_community_proxysql_proxysql_scheduler (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `filename` (String) Full path of the executable to be executed.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) A schedule with *active* set to `False` will be tracked in the database, but will be never loaded in the in-memory data structures.
- `arg1` (String) Argument that can be passed to the job.
- `arg2` (String) Argument that can be passed to the job.
- `arg3` (String) Argument that can be passed to the job.
- `arg4` (String) Argument that can be passed to the job.
- `arg5` (String) Argument that can be passed to the job.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `comment` (String) Text field that can be used for any purposed defined by the user.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `force_delete` (Boolean) By default we avoid deleting more than one schedule in a single batch, however if you need this behaviour and you are not concerned about the schedules deleted, you can set *force_delete* to `True`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interval_ms` (Number) How often (in millisecond) the job will be started. The minimum value for *interval_ms* is 100 milliseconds.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present` - adds the schedule, when `absent` - removes the schedule.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `stdout` (String) The schedule modified or removed from proxysql.
